notes: Each with-skill run nests a REAL 3-CLI council (claude+codex+agy headless), so these are expensive and need all three CLIs installed + authenticated. Prompts are deliberately the kind of high-stakes / ambiguous question where independent models may diverge — that's what makes synthesis worth grading. Assertions check that the orchestrator produced ONE integrated answer with attribution and conflict-handling, not three stapled summaries.

evals.name: architecture-decision-postgres-vs-sqlite
evals.prompt: Ask all three CLIs and give me the best combined answer: I'm building a small internal analytics tool that 4-5 teammates will use. It ingests about 50MB of CSV per day and runs occasional heavy aggregation queries (joins, window functions) for dashboards. Should I use SQLite or Postgres? I want a clear recommendation with the reasoning, not a fence-sit.
assertions: Gives a single clear recommendation (SQLite or Postgres), not a non-committal answer
Synthesizes into one integrated answer rather than three separate provider summaries pasted together
Attributes which provider(s) contributed what or where they agreed/disagreed
Addresses the concrete constraints from the prompt (multi-user concurrency, ~50MB/day, heavy aggregation queries)

evals.name: subtle-bug-review-consensus
assertions: Reaches a definitive verdict on dedupe_preserve_order rather than hedging
Correctly reasons about the `not seen.update(...)` side-effect trick (update returns None, so `not None` is True) and whether the dedupe actually works
Notes agreement or disagreement across the three providers and resolves it with reasoning rather than a blind majority vote
Produces one synthesized conclusion, not three separate provider analyses

evals.name: judgment-call-retry-policy
evals.prompt: I want a cross-model second opinion before I commit to this. We have a backend service that makes lots of outbound HTTP calls to internal and third-party APIs. A teammate wants to wrap EVERY outbound call in automatic retries with exponential backoff. Is that a good idea? Give me a balanced but decisive recommendation on where retries belong and where they don't.
assertions: Gives a decisive recommendation (not 'it depends' with no guidance) about where retries belong vs. don't
Raises the key risk that blanket retries on non-idempotent calls can cause duplicate side-effects / retry storms
Integrates the three responses into one answer and flags any disagreement between them
Notes which provider raised which point (attribution)
